title: Sushi Deck — Product Tour
summary: A sample deck that ships with the backend so there is something to present on day one. Neutral content — no client data.

slides.label: Cover
slides.title: Decks as *data*, served by one API
slides.sub: A portable presentation engine — authored as JSON, rendered by the kit, stored once.

blocks.headline: One backend, *many* consumers
blocks.lede: The library holds the deck logic; the API tier owns the database and the model; every front-end is a thin consumer.

items: *Author* decks as JSON — editable by non-developers.
*Generate* a first draft from a brief with Claude.
*Present* / scroll / print-to-PDF from the same source.
*Consume* over HTTP with a bearer key scoped to an owner.

blocks.headline: Three tiers, one source of truth

blocks.text: Both front-ends — the standalone app and moye-law-os — consume the *same* API. Neither touches Supabase or the LLM directly.

slides.label: Getting started
slides.disclaimer: Sample deck — replace with your own via the admin editor.

items: Open the admin editor and edit this deck, or create a new one.
Point a consumer at the API with a key that resolves to its owner.
Seed real content as DeckJson — decks stay owner-scoped and private.

blocks.text: Everything a recipient sees is rendered from the same DeckJson you edit.
